summary: Official Palantir JADC2 SDK example applications demonstrating how to build defense applications using the Palantir Defense Ontology and Gotham APIs. Contains two React+TypeScript apps: (1) Defense-Ontology-Explorer-App, which uses the OSDK to browse the Defense Ontology's six warfighting domains (Targeting and Fires, Protection, Sustainment, Mission Planning, Order of Battle, Intelligence) through interfaces, object types, and individual objects; and (2) gotham-api-explorer-app, which provides interactive OpenAPI-driven exploration of Gotham APIs (Geotime, Maps/Gaia, Target Workbench) with dynamic form generation and cURL export. Both apps authenticate via OAuth through Foundry Developer Console and use the OSDK client pattern of createClient(url, $ontologyRid, auth).

ontologyConcepts.evidence: The Defense Ontology is organized around abstract interfaces grouped by warfighting domain. The domainMappings.ts file enumerates 70+ interface names across 6 domains: 'Targeting and Fires' has interfaces like AttackGuidance, CollateralDamageAssessment, HighPayoffTarget, target; 'Intelligence' has Comint, Elint, Sigint, geoint, imint, mti, opir, osint; 'Sustainment' has EquipmentInterface, MaterielType, Unit, UnitHierarchy; etc. The explorer app drills from domain -> interface -> implementing object types -> objects, demonstrating that interfaces are the primary API abstraction layer of the Defense Ontology.

Objects are accessed via client(InterfaceType).asyncIter() to iterate through all objects implementing an interface. Each object has $objectType, $primaryKey, and $title properties. The fetchFullObjectSaga retrieves individual objects via client(Type).fetchOneWithErrors(primaryKey). Object types are the concrete data types that implement abstract Defense Ontology interfaces. The Domain.tsx component shows unique object types are extracted from interface results via obj.$objectType. The object namespace prefix 'com.palantir.defense.ontology.' is stripped for display via stripPrefix().

Object properties are rendered as raw JSON via JSON.stringify(selectedObject, null, 2) in the object detail view. The mediaReference property is special-cased: if an object has object.value.mediaReference, the app calls fetchContents() to load binary media (e.g., mil2525C symbology icons). This demonstrates that properties can include both primitive data and media attachments that require separate fetch calls.

The gotham-api-explorer-app demonstrates API actions via Gotham REST endpoints organized into three service categories: Geotime (write observations, search observation histories, link/unlink tracks and objects), Maps/Gaia (render objects, load tiles/symbols, search/load/export maps, add artifacts/objects/layers to maps), and Target Workbench (CRUD operations on targets, target boards, HPTLs, and intel). These operation IDs map directly to Gotham API actions with full OpenAPI spec-driven request/response handling.

The gotham-api-explorer-app fetches the full Gotham OpenAPI specification from /api/gotham/openapi (YAML format parsed via js-yaml) and dynamically generates forms from the spec. This demonstrates how Gotham exposes its entire API surface as an OpenAPI spec that can be consumed programmatically. The app supports complex schema features including $ref resolution, discriminated unions (oneOf with discriminator.mapping), nested objects, and arrays. Client scopes enumerate all data integration permissions: ontologies-read/write, admin-read/write, datasets-read/write, filesystem-read/write, map-read/write, target-read/write, geotime-read/write.

Both apps use OAuth2 public client authentication via @osdk/oauth createPublicOauthClient(). The gotham-api-explorer-app explicitly requests 14 OAuth scopes covering read/write for ontologies, admin, datasets, filesystem, maps, targets, and geotime. Access tokens are obtained via auth.refresh() and passed as Bearer tokens. The Defense Ontology Explorer requires specific Developer Console permissions including 'Media sets read permission' for rendering military symbology. CORS configuration is required in the Foundry Control Panel.

No explicit Ontology Functions (server-side TypeScript/Python functions) are demonstrated in this repo. The repo focuses on client-side OSDK queries and direct Gotham REST API calls rather than serverless ontology functions.

The Geotime API operations include explicit link/unlink operations (geotimeLinkTrackAndObject, geotimeLinkTracks, geotimeUnlinkTrackAndObject, geotimeUnlinkTracks) demonstrating track-to-object and track-to-track link relationships. The Defense Ontology includes UnitHierarchyLink and UnitHierarchyLinkType interfaces in the Sustainment domain, suggesting hierarchical link structures between military units.
No AI/LLM integration patterns are demonstrated in this repository. The apps focus on data browsing and API exploration rather than AI-driven workflows.

apiSurface.name: createClient
apiSurface.description: Creates an OSDK client instance from @osdk/client. Takes (url, $ontologyRid, auth) and returns a Client that can query ontology objects via client(Type).asyncIter() and client(Type).fetchOneWithErrors(primaryKey).

apiSurface.name: createPublicOauthClient
apiSurface.description: Creates a public OAuth client from @osdk/oauth. Takes (clientId, url, redirectUrl, [multiTab, undefined, windowLocation, scopes]). Supports signIn(), refresh() for token retrieval, and getTokenOrUndefined() for synchronous access.

apiSurface.name: $ontologyRid
apiSurface.description: Generated ontology resource identifier exported from the SDK package (e.g., @defense-ontology-explorer-app/sdk). Used as the second argument to createClient to identify which ontology to connect to.

apiSurface.name: client(InterfaceType).asyncIter()
apiSurface.description: Async iterator pattern for fetching all objects implementing a given interface type. Returns objects with $objectType, $primaryKey, and $title metadata properties. Used in for-await-of loops.

apiSurface.name: client(Type).fetchOneWithErrors
apiSurface.description: Fetches a single object by primary key with error handling. Returns a Result-like wrapper where the actual object is accessed via .value property.

apiSurface.name: mediaReference.fetchContents()
apiSurface.description: Fetches binary media content (e.g., symbology images) from an object's mediaReference property. Returns a Response-like object supporting .blob() for binary data. Requires 'Media sets read permission' scope.

apiSurface.name: /api/gotham/openapi
apiSurface.description: Gotham OpenAPI specification endpoint that returns a YAML document describing all available Gotham API endpoints including Geotime, Maps/Gaia, and Target Workbench operations with full schema definitions.

apiSurface.name: Geotime API
apiSurface.description: Gotham Geotime REST APIs under v1/observations and v1/tracks paths. Operations include writing observations, searching observation histories and latest observations, and linking/unlinking tracks and objects.

apiSurface.name: Maps/Gaia API
apiSurface.description: Gotham Maps/Gaia REST APIs under v1/maps and v1/maprendering paths. Operations include searching/loading maps, rendering objects and symbology, loading tiles, exporting KMZ, and adding artifacts/objects/layers to maps.

apiSurface.name: Target Workbench API
apiSurface.description: Gotham Target Workbench REST APIs under v1/twb path. Full CRUD operations on targets, target boards, high-priority target lists (HPTLs), and target intel with versioned V2 endpoints.

apiSurface.name: @osdk/foundry
apiSurface.description: Foundry Platform SDK used in gotham-api-explorer-app alongside @osdk/client. Provides lower-level Foundry platform access beyond the ontology-specific OSDK client.

architecturePatterns.description: The Defense Ontology organizes military data into six warfighting function domains inspired by CJADC2 doctrine: Targeting and Fires, Protection, Sustainment, Mission Planning, Order of Battle, and Intelligence. Each domain contains a set of abstract interfaces (70+ total) that concrete object types implement. This domain-interface-objectType hierarchy is the core architectural pattern, enabling a 'build once, deploy anywhere' approach where the same interface definitions work across different Foundry environments and classification networks.

Interfaces serve as the primary API contract layer. Concrete object types implement these interfaces, meaning the same code (e.g., client(AttackGuidance).asyncIter()) works regardless of the underlying object type or data source. The namespace convention 'com.palantir.defense.ontology.*' establishes a shared vocabulary across all Defense Ontology consumers.

The gotham-api-explorer-app fetches the Gotham OpenAPI spec at runtime and dynamically generates forms based on the specification. It handles complex schema features: $ref resolution for nested schemas, discriminated unions via oneOf+discriminator.mapping, arrays with add/remove operations, and enum dropdowns. This pattern allows the UI to adapt to any API changes without code modifications.

Both apps use Redux Toolkit with Redux Saga for managing OSDK interactions. Sagas handle async OSDK calls (asyncIter, fetchOneWithErrors) and manage loading/error states. The Defense app uses domain -> interface -> objectType -> object drill-down with distinct loading states for each level.

Both apps use foundry.config.json for deployment configuration targeting Foundry sites. They are configured as public client Developer Console applications with specific resource permissions. The apps can run locally (port 8080) or be deployed to Foundry using the site deployment pipeline with git-describe auto-versioning.

The gotham-api-explorer-app demonstrates the full scope of Gotham API permissions with 14 distinct OAuth scopes covering read/write access to ontologies, admin, datasets, filesystem, maps, targets, and geotime. This fine-grained scope model enables least-privilege access control for defense applications.

The Defense Ontology Explorer handles MIL-STD-2525C military symbology through the mediaReference property on objects and the mil2525CSymbol interface. Binary media content is fetched separately via fetchContents() and rendered as images. The Gotham Maps API also supports symbol rendering and tile loading for geospatial visualization.

The gotham-api-explorer-app generates formatted cURL commands from form inputs, enabling developers to reproduce API calls outside the app. This pattern is particularly valuable in defense contexts where developers may need to test API calls in different network environments or integrate with non-JavaScript toolchains.

typeDefinitions.name: OntologyCategory
typeDefinitions.definition: enum OntologyCategory { DEFENSE = 'Defense Ontology', NOTIONAL = 'Notional', YOUR = 'Your Ontology' }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Categorizes object types by their origin: Defense Ontology (standard Palantir interfaces), Notional (example/demo data), or Your Ontology (user's custom object types). Used to label and decorate tables in the explorer UI.

typeDefinitions.name: DomainCategory
typeDefinitions.definition: enum DomainCategory { targetingFires = 'Targeting and Fires', protection = 'Protection', sustainment = 'Sustainment', missionPlanning = 'Mission Planning', orderOfBattle = 'Order of Battle', intelligence = 'Intelligence' }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Enumerates the six warfighting function domains of the Defense Ontology, aligned with CJADC2 doctrine. Each domain groups related interfaces.

typeDefinitions.name: DomainMetadata
typeDefinitions.definition: interface DomainMetadata { title: string; description: string; interfaces: readonly string[]; status: string; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Metadata for each warfighting domain including human-readable title, description, list of interface API names, and publication status (COMING_SOON, UNDER_DEVELOPMENT, PUBLISHED).

typeDefinitions.name: OsdkState
typeDefinitions.definition: interface OsdkState { selectedDomain: DomainCategory | null; domainMap: { [key in DomainCategory]?: DomainMetadata }; errors: { fetchDomains: string | null; fetchInterfaceObjects: string | null; fetchFullObject: string | null; }; selectedInterface: string | null; selectedObjectType: string | null; selectedObjectPrimaryKey: string | null; selectedObject: (any & { mediaContent: string | null }) | null; interfaceObjects: any[]; loadingInterfaces: boolean; loadingObjectTypes: boolean; loadingObjects: boolean; loadingObjectDetails: boolean; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Full Redux state shape for the Defense Ontology Explorer. Tracks the drill-down selection hierarchy (domain -> interface -> objectType -> object) with independent loading and error states for each level.

typeDefinitions.name: DOMAIN_INTERFACE_MAPPINGS
typeDefinitions.definition: const DOMAIN_INTERFACE_MAPPINGS = { 'Targeting and Fires': ['AttackGuidance', 'ChangeAssessment', 'CollateralConcernCandidate', ...], 'Protection': [...], 'Sustainment': [...], 'Mission Planning': [...], 'Order of Battle': [...], 'Intelligence': [...] } as const
typeDefinitions.purpose: Auto-generated static mapping of warfighting domains to their Defense Ontology interface names. Contains 70+ interface names across 6 domains. This is the canonical enumeration of the Defense Ontology's interface surface area.

typeDefinitions.name: FormState
typeDefinitions.definition: interface FormState { endpoints: { [key: string]: any }; schemas: { [key: string]: any }; openapi: string | null; activeTab: string | null; activeMethod: string | null; fullPath: string; response: any; inputParams: { [key: string]: any }; inputValues: { [key: string]: any }; imageData: string | null; selectedUnionTypes: { [key: string]: any }; availableHeaders: string[]; paramFormErrors: { [key: string]: string }; requestFormErrors: { [key: string]: string }; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Redux state shape for the Gotham API explorer. Stores the parsed OpenAPI spec (endpoints, schemas), active endpoint selection, dynamically-built form input values, union type selections, and validation errors.

typeDefinitions.name: OperationMapping
typeDefinitions.purpose: Maps Gotham API operation IDs (e.g., 'targetWorkbenchCreateTargetV2') to human-readable operation names (e.g., 'Create a Target'). Covers 28 operations across Geotime, Maps, and Target Workbench services.

typeDefinitions.name: Defense Ontology Interface Names (Targeting and Fires)
typeDefinitions.definition: AttackGuidance, ChangeAssessment, CollateralConcernCandidate, CollateralConcernCandidateGeometry, CollateralConcernCandidatePoint, CollateralConcernList, CollateralDamageAssessment, FireSupportCoordinationMeasure, FunctionalAssessment, Guidance, HighPayoffTarget, HptTargetType, Location, MunitionEffectivenessAssessment, SourceSystemMetadata, TargetSelectionStandard, mil2525CSymbol, report, reportObservation, target
typeDefinitions.purpose: Interface names for the Targeting and Fires domain, covering the military targeting kill chain: target identification, attack guidance, fire support coordination measures, collateral damage assessment, and munition effectiveness assessment.

typeDefinitions.name: Defense Ontology Interface Names (Intelligence)
typeDefinitions.definition: BaseSatelliteImagery, Comint, Elint, FullSatelliteImagery, IntelReport, Intelligence, IntelligenceProducer, IntelligenceSubject, Location, OverheadCvDetection, OverheadImagery, Sigint, SourceSystemMetadata, emitterType, geoint, imint, mti, opir, osint, mil2525CSymbol, report, reportObservation
typeDefinitions.purpose: Interface names for the Intelligence domain, covering all intelligence disciplines: SIGINT (Comint, Elint, Sigint), GEOINT (geoint, satellite imagery, overhead CV detection), IMINT (imint, OverheadImagery), MTI (moving target indicator), OPIR (overhead persistent infrared), and OSINT (open-source intelligence).

typeDefinitions.name: Defense Ontology Interface Names (Sustainment)
typeDefinitions.definition: EquipmentInterface, EquipmentTypeInterface, Location, LogisticsStatusReport, Materiel, MaterielChangeEvent, MaterielRequirement, MaterielStatusEvent, MaterielTaxonomy, MaterielType, SourceSystemMetadata, SupplyLocation, Unit, UnitHierarchy, UnitHierarchyGroup, UnitHierarchyLink, UnitHierarchyLinkType, UnitType, mil2525CSymbol, report, reportObservation
typeDefinitions.purpose: Interface names for the Sustainment domain, covering logistics and unit management: materiel lifecycle (requirements, status events, taxonomy), unit organization (hierarchy, types, links), supply locations, and equipment tracking.

typeDefinitions.name: Defense Ontology Interface Names (Mission Planning)
typeDefinitions.definition: FollowRouteTask, Location, MovementProfile, Objective, Plan, PositionTask, SourceSystemMetadata, TargetTask, Task, TaskMaterielRequirement, TaskMaterielType, TaskableEntity, TaskableEntityType, capability, mil2525CSymbol, report, reportObservation, route
typeDefinitions.purpose: Interface names for the Mission Planning domain, covering task-based planning: plans with objectives, tasks (follow route, position, target), taskable entities with capabilities, movement profiles, routes, and materiel requirements per task.

typeDefinitions.name: Defense Ontology Interface Names (Order of Battle)
typeDefinitions.definition: Facility, FacilityV2, Location, SourceSystemMetadata, defenseGeotemporalObservation, defenseTrackedEntity, equipment, equipmentType, mil2525CSymbol, report, reportObservation, weapon, weaponType
typeDefinitions.purpose: Interface names for the Order of Battle domain, covering force structure: facilities, tracked entities with geotemporal observations, equipment and weapon types, and versioned facility types (FacilityV2).

dependencies.name: @osdk/client
dependencies.purpose: Core OSDK client library for querying ontology objects. Provides createClient(), asyncIter(), fetchOneWithErrors(), and other object query methods.

dependencies.name: @osdk/oauth
dependencies.purpose: OAuth2 authentication client for Foundry. Provides createPublicOauthClient() for browser-based public client OAuth flows with signIn(), refresh(), and getTokenOrUndefined().

dependencies.name: @osdk/foundry
dependencies.purpose: Foundry Platform SDK providing lower-level platform access. Used in gotham-api-explorer-app alongside the ontology-specific OSDK client.

dependencies.name: @defense-ontology-explorer-app/sdk
dependencies.purpose: Generated OSDK package containing Defense Ontology type definitions and $ontologyRid. Provides typed interface and object type exports for type-safe OSDK queries.

dependencies.name: @gotham-api-explorer-app/sdk
dependencies.purpose: Generated OSDK package for the Gotham API explorer app. Exports $ontologyRid for client initialization.

dependencies.name: @blueprintjs/core
dependencies.purpose: Palantir's Blueprint UI component library providing buttons, icons, spinners, toasters, and other UI components used throughout both apps.

dependencies.name: @reduxjs/toolkit
dependencies.purpose: Redux state management with createSlice pattern. Both apps use RTK for managing OSDK state (domains, interfaces, objects) and form state (endpoints, schemas, inputs).

dependencies.name: redux-saga
dependencies.purpose: Side effect management for async OSDK queries and form processing. Sagas handle fetching domains, interface objects, full objects, and updating form values.

dependencies.name: js-yaml
dependencies.purpose: YAML parser used in gotham-api-explorer-app to parse the Gotham OpenAPI spec returned from /api/gotham/openapi endpoint.

dependencies.name: react-markdown
dependencies.purpose: Renders Markdown descriptions from OpenAPI spec endpoint documentation within the API explorer UI.

dependencies.name: react
dependencies.purpose: React 18 as the UI framework for both applications.

dependencies.name: vite
dependencies.purpose: Build tool and dev server for both applications, configured to run on port 8080.

dependencies.name: typescript
dependencies.purpose: TypeScript compiler for type-safe development across both applications.
